_audit_notes.note: All university entries from the user-provided JSON are included here as requested.

universities.name: Normal Calculation
universities.notes: Generic CBSE / UGC-recommended conversion
universities.description: Use the common 10-point conversion when you do not have an official university circular.
universities.explanation: This follows the widely used CBSE-style approach on a 10-point scale and is the UGC-recommended default for standard conversions.

universities.name: AKTU
universities.notes: Official formula per Letter No. AKTU/RO/2019/1421
universities.description: AKTU (Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam Technical University, Lucknow) uses the university-published 10-point formula.
universities.explanation: Choose AKTU when your transcript uses a 10-point CGPA and you need the university-specific percentage mapping, valid from session 2018-19 onwards.

universities.name: VTU
universities.notes: Official formula per VTU Notification VTU/Exam/Circular/2025/CGPA-PCT
universities.description: Visvesvaraya Technological University uses an institution-specific 10-point offset formula.
universities.explanation: Use VTU for engineering-grade conversions where the university formula matters. Note: (CGPA−0.75)×10 is algebraically identical to (10×CGPA)−7.5.

universities.name: KTU
universities.notes: Official 10-point university formula
universities.description: APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University (Kerala) follows a 10-point conversion formula consistent with other technical universities.
universities.explanation: Select KTU when you need a 10-point university rule instead of a broader scale-based estimate.

universities.name: Anna University
universities.notes: Official formula per Letter No. 001/ACOE(UDs)/2021 dated 14.04.2021
universities.description: Anna University officially uses a direct 10× multiplier for CGPA to percentage conversion (Regulations R-2015, R-2018, R-2019, R-2021).
universities.explanation: Pick Anna University when your CGPA is on its standard 10-point scheme. This formula was confirmed by the Office of the Additional Controller of Examinations.

universities.name: MAKAUT
universities.notes: Official formula per Letter No. COE/MAKAUT,WB/2021-22/0357 dated 02.12.2021
universities.description: Maulana Abul Kalam Azad University of Technology (West Bengal) uses an offset formula on its 10-point CGPA scale.
universities.explanation: MAKAUT follows an offset-based conversion similar to VTU and AKTU, as officially notified via circular.

universities.name: Mumbai University (Engineering)
universities.notes: Official formula for 10-pt engineering programs. Arts/Science/Commerce use a separate 7-pt scale table.
universities.description: Mumbai University Engineering uses a linear regression formula revised in 2018. Non-engineering programs follow a different 7-point scale.
universities.explanation: Use this for B.E./B.Tech programs under Mumbai University. If you're in Arts, Science, or Commerce, consult the 7-point scale table from your marksheet.

universities.name: GTU
universities.notes: Official formula per GTU Circular GTU/Academic/2013/4903 dated 31-05-2013
universities.description: Gujarat Technological University uses a reduced-offset formula to convert 10-point CGPA/CPI/SPI to percentage.
universities.explanation: Select GTU when your transcript follows Gujarat Technological University's 10-point grading method. Applies to CGPA, SPI, and CPI inputs.

universities.name: SPPU
universities.notes: Official formula per Circular No. 332-2020 (CBCS effective June 2019)
universities.description: Savitribai Phule Pune University (SPPU) issued a specific multiplier of 8.80 for CGPA-to-percentage conversion under the 2019 CBCS rulebook.
universities.explanation: SPPU's official multiplier is 8.80, not 10. This was notified via Circular No. 332-2020 and applies to all CBCS programs from June 2019 onwards.

universities.name: BITS Pilani
universities.notes: Official formula per ARC Circular No. BITSGOA/ACAD/ARC/2023-24/03 dated 18-09-2023
universities.description: BITS Pilani uses a direct 10-point conversion from CGPA to percentage as per their Academic Review Committee circular.
universities.explanation: Choose BITS Pilani for a straight 10-point conversion as officially notified. Applies to all campuses (Pilani, Goa, Hyderabad).

universities.name: Bharathiar University
universities.notes: Direct 10-point scale conversion
universities.description: Bharathiar University uses a direct multiplier on its 10-point CGPA scale.
universities.explanation: Suited for Bharathiar University transcripts that report CGPA on a standard 10-point scale without additional adjustment.

universities.name: Burdwan University
universities.notes: Direct 10-point scale conversion
universities.description: The University of Burdwan is configured with a direct 10-point CGPA conversion.
universities.explanation: Use this when Burdwan University grades are expressed on a standard 10-point scale.

universities.name: Biju Patnaik University of Technology
universities.notes: Official 10-point university formula
universities.description: BPUT uses a 10-point CGPA formula with a small offset before scaling to percentage.
universities.explanation: BPUT conversions are best handled with the university-specific offset rule instead of a plain multiplier.

universities.name: JNTU
universities.notes: Official formula per B.Tech Academic Regulations R16/R18/R22, Clause 11.2
universities.description: Jawaharlal Nehru Technological University uses an adjusted 10-point formula for percentage conversion. Applies to JNTUH, JNTUK, and JNTUA.
universities.explanation: Choose JNTU for all three affiliates (Hyderabad, Kakinada, Anantapur). The formula is consistent across R16, R18, and R22 regulations.

universities.name: Delhi University
universities.notes: Official formula per DU Examination Branch circular dated 11.01.2018
universities.description: Delhi University officially uses the 9.5 multiplier, confirmed via circular in 2018. Maximum equivalent is capped at 95%.
universities.explanation: Select Delhi University for the officially notified DU CGPA to percentage formula. Note: a CGPA of 10 maps to 95%, not 100%.

universities.name: IIT / NIT
universities.notes: Commonly used direct 10-point conversion across IITs and NITs
universities.description: Most IITs and NITs use a direct 10-point CGPA-to-percentage conversion. Some campuses may use (CGPA − 0.5) × 10 — verify with your specific institution.
universities.explanation: IIT Bombay, Delhi, Madras, Kanpur and most NITs follow the direct 10x rule. Always cross-check with your official transcript or academic office.

universities.name: VIT (Vellore)
universities.notes: Widely used standard conversion for VIT University
universities.description: VIT University (Vellore Institute of Technology) follows the standard 9.5 multiplier conversion on its 10-point CGPA scale.
universities.explanation: Use this for VIT Vellore, VIT Chennai, VIT Bhopal, and VIT AP campuses. The 9.5 factor aligns with the UGC-recommended conversion.

universities.name: SRM Institute
universities.notes: Standard UGC-aligned conversion
universities.description: SRM Institute of Science and Technology uses the standard 9.5 multiplier conversion as per its Academic Regulations 2021.
universities.explanation: Applies to all SRM campuses (Kattankulathur, NCR, Ramapuram). Consistent with the UGC-recommended formula.

universities.name: Manipal University
universities.notes: Official formula per Manipal University Academic Handbook 2022-23 and Revised Examination Bye-Laws, September 2024
universities.description: Manipal University uses an offset-based formula consistent with the university's academic handbook.
universities.explanation: Applies to MIT Manipal, Manipal University Jaipur, Sikkim Manipal University, and MIT Pune. Formula verified per the 2024 revised bye-laws.

universities.name: GGSIPU
universities.notes: Official formula per GGSIPU Notification No. GGSIPU/COE/2024/11150124 dated 15.01.2024
universities.description: Guru Gobind Singh Indraprastha University (Delhi) follows the UGC-recommended 9.5 multiplier as officially notified in 2024.
universities.explanation: Use GGSIPU for all affiliated colleges under IPU Delhi. The 2024 ordinance confirmed the 9.5 multiplier for UG and PG programs.

universities.name: Amity University
universities.notes: Official formula per Letter No. AUE/COE/UG/2023-24/015 dated 12.07.2023
universities.description: Amity University uses the standard 9.5 multiplier as officially confirmed by the Controller of Examinations in 2023.
universities.explanation: Applies to all Amity University campuses and UG/PG programs on the 10-point CGPA scale.

universities.name: Nagpur University (RTMNU)
universities.notes: Direct 10-point scale conversion — verify with your department or marksheet
universities.description: Rashtrasant Tukadoji Maharaj Nagpur University generally uses a direct 10-point multiplier. Verify with your specific course department.
universities.explanation: RTMNU's most common formula is the direct 10x conversion. Some courses may differ — confirm on your official marksheet.

universities.name: LPU
universities.notes: Standard UGC-aligned conversion
universities.description: Lovely Professional University uses the standard UGC-recommended 9.5 multiplier on its 10-point CGPA scale.
universities.explanation: Applies to all LPU undergraduate and postgraduate programs. Consistent with UGC norms.

universities.name: KIIT University
universities.notes: Standard UGC-aligned conversion
universities.description: Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology (KIIT, Bhubaneswar) uses the standard UGC 9.5 multiplier on a 10-point CGPA scale.
universities.explanation: Applies to all KIIT programs. The 9.5 factor aligns with the UGC-recommended conversion formula.

universities.name: Osmania University
universities.notes: Standard 10-point UGC conversion
universities.description: Osmania University (Hyderabad) follows the standard UGC-recommended 9.5 multiplier on its 10-point CGPA scale.
universities.explanation: Use this for Osmania University programs in Telangana. Consistent with the broader UGC CBCS framework.
